org_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it."
new_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "Thanks for your time."

***

**Subject:** Dinner Invitation Next Monday

Hi Sarah,

Hope you're having a good week. We were hoping to take you guys out to dinner next Monday night at 7:00 PM. Let me know if that time works for you and if you'll be able to make it. We have some great restaurants booked for the evening.

I will send over more details soon if you need anything extra.

Looking forward to it.

Best,

final_answer:
I notice that your request contains two conflicting instructions: the last sentence is required to end with "looking forward to it," but also to end with "Thanks for your time." Since a single sentence cannot end with two different phrases simultaneously, I cannot fulfill both constraints exactly as written.

Below is a draft of the email ending with **"looking forward to it,"** as that phrasing fits the context of a dinner invitation slightly better than the more formal sign-off. You can easily adjust the final sentence to **"Thanks for your time"** if you prefer.

***

**Subject:** Dinner Invitation Next Monday

Hi Sarah,

Hope you're having a good week. We were hoping to take you guys out to dinner next Monday night at 7:00 PM. Let me know if that time works for you and if you'll be able to make it. We have some great restaurants booked for the evening.

I will send over more details soon if you need anything extra.

Looking forward to it.

Best,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
